questions.question: POEM 3 Mission, recently in the news, is launched by which space organization?
questions.explanation: ISRO’s PSLV Orbital Experimental Module 3 (POEM 3) Mission aims to tackle orbital debris with PSLV’s PS4 stage, designated as the PSLV Orbital Experimental Module. This marks ISRO’s third deployment of PS4 for orbital experiments, dubbed POEM-3. The module will conduct in-orbit scientific experiments for 4-6 months, powered by solar panels and a Lithium-ion battery. Equipped with 10 payloads, it aims to explore microgravity conditions and advance space research.

questions.question: Recently, which valley hosting the largest nesting colony for Gyps vultures in southern India?
questions.explanation: The Moyar Valley, also known as Maayar Valley, hosts the critically endangered Gyps Vulture. Located within the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve, stretching from Gudalur through Mudumalai Tiger Reserve, it harbors vital species like tigers and elephants. This region boasts the largest nesting colony of Gyps vultures in peninsular India. Biomes, classified by resident species, define this area, making it a crucial habitat for endangered wildlife.

questions.question: START 2024, a space program, recently launched by which organization?
questions.explanation: ISRO’s START 2024 program is an online training program that teaches space science and technology to undergraduate and postgraduate science and technology students. The program is scheduled to take place in April and May 2024. The main goal of the program is to attract young students to space science and technology.

questions.question: Recently, who has been appointed as the Director General of National Investigation Agency (NIA)?
questions.explanation: The central government recently appointed new Director Generals for key agencies. Sadanand Vasant Date, a 1990 batch IPS officer from Maharashtra, now heads the National Investigation Agency (NIA). Rajeev Kumar Sharma, also from the 1990 batch but from Rajasthan cadre, will oversee the Bureau of Police Research & Development (BPRD).

questions.question: Recently, which country poised to become the first Southeast Asia country to allow same-sex marriage?
questions.explanation: Thailand’s lower house of parliament approved a marriage equality bill, signaling a step towards legalizing same-sex marriage in Southeast Asia. Supported by 400 out of 415 lawmakers, it awaits approval from the upper house. If passed, Thailand would be the third Asian nation to legalize gay marriage. This historic decision marks a significant milestone in the region’s LGBTQ+ rights movement.

questions.question: Recently, where was the world’s first Om-shaped temple inaugurated?
questions.explanation: The world’s first Om-shaped temple, ‘Om Aakar’, inaugurated in Jadan, Rajasthan’s Pali district, spans 250 acres. Its Nagara style and intricate design honor North India’s heritage. Holding 1,008 idols of Lord Mahadev and 12 Jyotirlingas, it stands at 135 ft with 2,000 pillars and 108 rooms. The top has a shivling made of rhinestone from Dholpur. A 2 lakh tonne tank enhances its grandeur, visible from space.

questions.question: Tantalum, a rare metal, was recently discovered in which river?
questions.explanation: Traces of tantalum, a rare metal crucial in electronics, were found in the Sutlej river by Indian Institute of Technology, Punjab researchers. Tantalum is vital for capacitors and resistors in devices like smartphones and laptops. Its discovery hints at untapped mineral wealth beneath the riverbed, sparking interest in further exploration. Tantalum’s unique properties make it valuable, potentially impacting various industries and the local economy. Further surveys are needed to assess the extent of the deposits.

questions.question: NHPC, India’s largest hydropower utility, recently secured a loan from which country for renewable projects in Bikaner, Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: NHPC Limited, India’s largest hydropower utility, secured a JPY 20.00 billion loan from Japan Bank for International Cooperation (JBIC) for renewable energy projects. This includes a 300 MW solar project in Bikaner under the CPSU 1000 MW Scheme. JBIC’s GREEN Operations program supports environmental preservation. The loan, facilitated by MUFG Bank Ltd Japan and Bank of Yokohama Ltd Japan, marks NHPC’s first foray into JBIC financing for renewables. The Bikaner Solar Project aligns with India’s aim for Net Zero emissions by 2070.

questions.question: United Nations Secretary-General has appointed which Indian as his Special Representative for Disaster Risk Reduction?
questions.explanation: India’s National Disaster Management Authority official, Kamal Kishore, appointed by UN Secretary-General António Guterres as special representative for disaster risk reduction. Kishore (55), currently serving as NDMA’s secretary to the government of India, succeeds Mami Mizutori. Appointed assistant secretary-general and special representative of the secretary-general for disaster risk reduction at UNDRR, Kishore’s role is crucial in global disaster mitigation efforts, announced by the UN spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric.

questions.question: Gyalsung project, which was seen in news, is associated with which country?
questions.explanation: India disbursed the second installment of ₹500 crore to Bhutan for infrastructure development under the GyalSung Project. Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s recent visit to Bhutan, concluded on March 23, and emphasized India’s commitment to Bhutan’s development. Modi pledged ₹10,000 crore over five years to Bhutan. This reaffirms India’s support for its Himalayan neighbor’s growth, strengthening bilateral ties and regional cooperation.
